---
title: "STA313 A2"
author: "Tala, Jinny"
date: "2026-03-06"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r, load data sets. echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
jobs <- read.csv('Jobs.csv')
participants <- read.csv('Participants.csv')
pubs <- read.csv ('Pubs.csv')
rest <- read.csv('Restaurants.csv')
finjour <- read.csv('Data/FinancialJournal.csv')
logs <- read_csv("Data/ParticipantStatusLogs1.csv")
```

```{r, load libraries, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
```

```{r -  EDA #1 }
# Exploring whether the social places are underused relative to their capacity:

pubs <- pubs %>%
  separate(location, into=c("lon","lat"), sep=",") %>%
  mutate(
    lon = as.numeric(lon),
    lat = as.numeric(lat)
  )

ggplot(pubs, aes(x=lon, y=lat)) +
  geom_point(aes(size=maxOccupancy), alpha=.7, color="darkred") +
  scale_size_continuous(name="Max Occupancy") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title="Where Social Spaces Exist",
    subtitle="Pubs sized by maximum occupancy"
  )

```

```{r - EDA #2}
# Where do people spend most of their time?
mode_counts <- logs %>%
  count(currentMode) %>%
  mutate(prop = n/sum(n))

ggplot(mode_counts, aes(x=reorder(currentMode, prop), y=prop)) +
  geom_col(fill="steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title="Where Participants Spend Their Time",
    y="Proportion of Time",
    x=""
  )

# Least amount of time spent in restaurants, could it be becasue its too expensive?
```

```{r}
restaurants <- restaurants %>%
  mutate(
    lon = as.numeric(str_extract(location, "-?\\d+\\.\\d+(?= )")),
    lat = as.numeric(str_extract(location, "-?\\d+\\.\\d+$"))
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data=pubs, aes(lon,lat), color="red", alpha=.6) +
  geom_point(data=restaurants, aes(lon,lat), color="orange", alpha=.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title="Social Venues in Engagement City")


library(readr)

pubs2 <- read_csv(
  "Pubs.csv",
  col_types = cols(location = col_character())
)
```
